components.name: accordion
components.description: Stacked disclosure panels that expand and collapse on click or keyboard, with optional multi-open mode.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-allow-multi
dataAttributes.description: When present, multiple items can stay open at once; otherwise opening one closes the others

detail.open: boolean (new state of the toggled item)

aria: aria-controls (on head, points to body id)
a11y.notes: Heads default to <button type="button">. Bodies receive an auto-generated id when none is set. The brut:change event bubbles from the toggled item, not the accordion root.

examples.title: Default — single-open
examples.html:

examples.html:

Second panel body.

components.name: alert
components.description: Notification banner with icon slot and semantic color variants for success, warning, error, and info states.
components.notes: Use the .brut-alert__icon child element for the icon slot. Without a status modifier, the alert uses the default bone background. Always include a modifier to convey meaning.

examples.title: Success alert
examples.html:
✓
Operation completed successfully.

examples.title: Error alert
examples.html:
!
Something went wrong. Please try again.

components.name: body
components.description: Base body-text typography class that sets font-family, font-size (base scale), line-height, and a 70ch max-width for comfortable reading measure.
components.notes:
This is a TYPOGRAPHY class for inline/block text containers — NOT for the HTML element. Apply it to

,

, or
elements that hold body copy. The tag itself does not need any BRUT class.

examples.title: Paragraph text
examples.html: Your paragraph content here. The 70ch max-width ensures comfortable reading measure without needing a wrapper.

examples.title: Card description
examples.html:
Title

Description text styled at base size with optimal line length.

components.name: btn
components.description: Brutalist button with thick border, shadow, and hover lift effect. Works on button, anchor, and input elements.

modifiers.name: --sm
modifiers.description: Small size — reduced padding and font size

modifiers.name: --lg
modifiers.description: Large size — increased padding and font size

components.notes: The base .brut-btn uses bone background with ink text. Color modifiers override only the background. Size modifiers override padding, font-size, and border-width. Combine freely: .brut-btn .brut-btn--primary .brut-btn--lg.

examples.title: Primary action button
examples.html: <button class="brut-btn brut-btn--primary">Get Started</button>

examples.title: Button group with variants
Link styled as button
examples.html: Read Docs

components.name: card
components.description: Bordered content container with background, shadow, and padding. A general-purpose surface for grouping related content.
components.notes: Cards have no header/body/footer sub-elements built in. Compose with .brut-stack for vertical spacing inside, or nest headings and body text directly. Cards are block-level by default.

components.name: carousel
components.description: Single-track slide carousel with prev/next, dots, keyboard, autoplay, optional loop, and pointer-driven swipe.

dataAttributes.name: data-current
dataAttributes.description: Initial slide index; clamped to slide count

dataAttributes.name: data-autoplay
dataAttributes.description: Autoplay interval in milliseconds

dataAttributes.name: data-loop
dataAttributes.description: When present, prev/next wrap at edges instead of clamping

a11y.notes: Autoplay respects prefers-reduced-motion and pauses on hover, focus, and tab visibility change. Arrow keys are RTL-aware.

components.name: checkbox
components.description: Visual checkbox label whose checked state is mirrored by an inner hidden <input type="checkbox">.

formState.name: Wraps a real <input type="checkbox" hidden> which carries name/value for native form submission

a11y.notes: Suppresses the native <label> click so the inner input toggles exactly once. Works without an inner input by toggling the .brut-checkbox--on class directly.

examples.title: Checked and unchecked
examples.html:
<label class="brut-checkbox" data-brut="checkbox"><input type="checkbox" hidden checked/></label>
<label class="brut-checkbox" data-brut="checkbox"><input type="checkbox" hidden/></label>

examples.title: With form name
examples.html:
<label class="brut-checkbox" data-brut="checkbox">
  <input type="checkbox" name="agree" value="yes" hidden/>
</label>

components.name: cluster
components.description: Horizontal flex layout with wrapping and configurable gap. The primary primitive for inline/horizontal grouping of elements.
components.notes: Use .brut-cluster--center to horizontally center a group of items. For centering a single block of content on the page, prefer .brut-container (auto margins) or the utility classes .d-flex + .justify-content-center + .align-items-center.

examples.title: Button group
Centered content
Space-between layout
examples.html: Left content Right content

components.name: combobox
components.description: Searchable single-select with a text input that filters a static option list and a hidden input mirror for form submission.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the auto-injected hidden input when one is not supplied in markup

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Value written to the hidden input when this option is picked; falls back to option text content

formState.name: Hidden input is created automatically when data-brut-name is set; otherwise consumer supplies <input type="hidden">

a11y.role: combobox (on the text input); listbox (on the list); option (on each item)
a11y.notes: Outside click closes the list. Filter is a case-insensitive substring match on option text. The .brut-combobox__empty element is shown when no options match.

components.name: container
components.description: Centered content wrapper with horizontal padding and a configurable max-width. Centers itself with auto margins.
components.notes: The base .brut-container has a default max-width. Use modifiers to override. Nesting containers is valid for narrowing content within a wider section.

examples.title: Default page wrapper
examples.html:
Page Title

Content constrained to readable width.

examples.title: Narrow content area
examples.html: Narrow column for focused reading.

components.name: counter
components.description: Live character counter that mirrors the length of a paired input or textarea and flags overflow.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-for
dataAttributes.description: Required — id of the element whose value length is counted

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-max
dataAttributes.description: Optional fallback maximum when the target has no maxlength attribute

a11y.notes: Read-only indicator; not focusable. Pair the target input with an aria-describedby pointing at the counter element if the count should be announced.

examples.title: Textarea with maxlength
Override max via data attribute

components.name: date
components.description: Text field paired with a popover calendar; selecting a day writes ISO YYYY-MM-DD into the visible field and a hidden mirror.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the auto-injected hidden input when one is not supplied in markup

formState.name: Hidden input is created automatically when data-brut-name is set; otherwise consumer supplies <input type="hidden">

a11y.role: combobox (on the field); grid (on the day grid); gridcell (on each day)
a11y.notes: Week starts on Monday. Outside-month days are rendered but visually muted via --out. Outside-click closes the popover. PageUp/PageDown shift the visible month and clamp the focused day to the new month's last day.

examples.title: Empty due date
Pre-filled value

components.name: dialog
components.description: Modal dialog wired to external open triggers with optional scrim, escape-to-close, and any [data-brut-close] inside.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-scrim
dataAttributes.description: Optional sibling scrim toggled in lockstep with the dialog

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-open
dataAttributes.description: Click target that opens the dialog with the matching id

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: Marks any element inside the dialog as a close trigger

a11y.notes: Toggles the [hidden] attribute rather than display:none. Scrim closes only on direct click (not on bubbling from inner content). The dialog id is required — components without an id are skipped.

examples.title: Confirm dialog with scrim
examples.html:
DELETE PROJECT?
This nukes project-alpha and all its history.

components.name: display
components.description: Hero/poster headline typography with fluid responsive sizing, uppercase transform, and tight line-height. Available ONLY as numbered variants — there is no base .brut-display class.
components.notes: There is NO standalone .brut-display class. You MUST use a numbered variant: .brut-display-1, .brut-display-2, or .brut-display-3. Using .brut-display alone will have no effect.

examples.title: Hero headline (largest)
examples.html: BUILD SOMETHING BRUTAL

examples.title: Section title (medium)
examples.html: OUR FEATURES

examples.title: Subsection title (smallest)
examples.html: GET STARTED

components.name: drawer
components.description: Side sheet that slides in from any edge, opened by external triggers with optional scrim and escape-to-close.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-side
dataAttributes.description: Edge the drawer slides from; auto-applied as a .brut-drawer--<side> class if missing

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-scrim
dataAttributes.description: Optional sibling scrim toggled in lockstep with the drawer

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-open
dataAttributes.description: Click target that opens the drawer with the matching id

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: Marks any element inside the drawer as a close trigger

a11y.notes: Forces a layout flush before adding --open so the slide transition runs from the closed transform. The drawer id is required — components without an id are skipped.

examples.title: Right drawer
examples.html:
RIGHT DRAWER

This drawer slides in from the right edge.

examples.title: Bottom sheet
examples.html:
BOTTOM DRAWER

Bottom drawer is full-width and clamped to 80vh.

components.name: dropzone
components.description: Drag-and-drop file region wrapping a hidden <input type="file">, with click-to-browse, keyboard activation, and a drag-over state class.

formState.name: Wraps a real <input type="file"> — its name attribute carries the upload field on form submission

a11y.notes: Sets role="button" and tabindex="0" if absent. Programmatic FileList assignment uses a DataTransfer to satisfy browsers that reject direct .files writes; the inner input also fires a native change event.

examples.title: Single file
Multiple files with accept filter

components.name: file
components.description: File picker that wraps a hidden <input type="file"> with a visible button and live filename label.

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input type="file">

a11y.notes: Uses a real <label> wrapping the native file input, so click, focus, and keyboard activation are inherited from the browser. The visible button is purely decorative.

examples.title: Single file
Multiple files

components.name: grid
components.description: CSS Grid layout with equal-width columns. Requires a column-count modifier to define the grid structure.
components.notes: The base .brut-grid sets display: grid with a default gap but NO column template. You MUST add a column modifier (--2, --3, --4, or --6) to define the layout. For a responsive 12-column system, use .brut-row with .brut-col-* children instead.

examples.html:

components.name: menu
components.description: Trigger-anchored dropdown menu with click-outside, Escape, and arrow-key focus navigation.

modifiers.name: .brut-menu__item--danger
modifiers.description: Destructive action style for an item (e.g. Delete, Sign out)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-menu-open
dataAttributes.description: Placed on a trigger element; clicking it toggles the matching menu

a11y.notes: Trigger gets type="button" and aria-haspopup automatically. Escape closes the menu and returns focus to the trigger. Clicking any .brut-menu__item closes the menu. Position recomputes on resize and scroll.

examples.title: Row actions menu
Overflow menu with link items
examples.html:

components.name: multiselect
components.description: Combobox-style field that holds chips for selected values with type-to-filter, keyboard toggle, and Backspace removal.

modifiers.name: .brut-multiselect--open
modifiers.description: Applied while the option list is visible

modifiers.name: .brut-multiselect__opt--selected
modifiers.description: Marks an option as currently chosen (toggled internally)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute used on the generated hidden inputs (one per selected value)

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: On each .brut-multiselect__opt, the value submitted when selected

dataAttributes.name: data-selected
dataAttributes.description: On a .brut-multiselect__opt, marks it as pre-selected on init

formState.name: one <input type="hidden" name="<data-brut-name>"> per selected value, marked with data-brut-mirror="1"

keyboard: Backspace (remove last chip when input empty)
a11y.notes: Click outside closes the list. Filtering hides non-matching options and shows the .brut-multiselect__empty fallback when no options match.

components.name: otp
components.description: One-time-code input rendered as one cell per digit with auto-advance, paste-fill, and Backspace/Arrow navigation.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-len
dataAttributes.description: Number of cells to auto-generate when none are present

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute on the hidden input that mirrors the joined value

detail.value: string (full code, fires when all cells are filled)

formState.name: auto-created <input type="hidden" name="<data-brut-name>"> with the joined value

a11y.notes: Cells are real <input> elements with inputmode="numeric" and autocomplete="one-time-code", so iOS/Android suggest SMS codes. Non-digit keystrokes are stripped. Pasting a longer string fills cells left-to-right from the focused cell.

examples.title: 6-digit, auto-generated cells
4-digit PIN
Pre-populated cells

components.name: pagination
components.description: Page navigation with prev/next, numbered buttons, first/last anchors, and ellipsis gaps for large page counts.

dataAttributes.name: data-total
dataAttributes.description: Total number of items in the dataset

dataAttributes.name: data-page-size
dataAttributes.description: Number of items shown per page

dataAttributes.name: data-page
dataAttributes.description: Current 1-based page; clamped to total page count

dataAttributes.name: data-sibling-count
dataAttributes.description: Pages shown on each side of the current page before collapsing into an ellipsis

a11y.notes: Root gets role="navigation" and aria-label="Pagination" if absent. Active page button carries aria-current="page". Prev/next buttons are disabled at the edges. Ellipsis gaps are aria-hidden so screen readers skip them.

examples.title: Default — 10 pages of 20
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="200" data-page-size="20" data-page="1"></nav>

examples.title: Mid-range with siblings collapsed
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="500" data-page-size="25" data-page="10" data-sibling-count="1"></nav>

examples.title: Wider sibling window
examples.html: <nav class="brut-pagination" data-brut="pagination" data-total="1000" data-page-size="50" data-page="7" data-sibling-count="2"></nav>

components.name: password
components.description: Password input with a SHOW/HIDE toggle button that flips the input type between password and text.

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input> (type flips between "password" and "text")

a11y.notes: Toggle button is forced to type="button" so it never submits the surrounding form. Label text and aria attributes update in sync with the visibility state.

examples.title: Default (hidden)
With initial value

components.name: popover
components.description: Trigger-anchored card with optional head bar, dismissed by click-outside, Escape, or any [data-brut-close] inside.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-popover-open
dataAttributes.description: On a trigger element; clicking it toggles the matching popover

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-close
dataAttributes.description: On any element inside the popover; clicking it closes the popover

a11y.notes: Trigger gets type="button" automatically. Click outside or pressing Escape closes the popover. Position recomputes on window resize and capture-phase scroll so it stays anchored to the trigger.

examples.title: Trigger + popover with head
examples.html: Filters

examples.title: Plain body, no head
examples.html: A popover is a small anchored card. Click the trigger again, click outside, or press Esc to close.

components.name: progress
components.description: Determinate or indeterminate horizontal progress bar driven by a CSS --progress variable, with a JS API for live updates.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value
dataAttributes.description: Initial progress value; mirrored to aria-valuenow and the --progress CSS variable

aria: aria-valuenow (kept in sync with current value)
a11y.notes: role and aria-valuemin/max are set on init. Use el.brutProgress.setValue(n) and el.brutProgress.getValue() for programmatic updates. The optional .brut-progress__label text is updated to the rounded percentage on every setValue.

examples.title: Static determinate (CSS-only via --progress)
JS-driven with label
Indeterminate

components.name: radio
components.description: Visual radio control synced to an inner hidden <input type="radio">, grouped by name, with Space/Enter activation.

modifiers.name: .brut-radio--on
modifiers.description: Checked visual state (toggled in sync with the underlying input)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Optional group name when there is no inner radio input; siblings sharing this name behave as one group

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Value emitted in brut:change when there is no inner radio input

formState.name: submits via the wrapped native <input type="radio">

a11y.notes: role="radio" and tabindex="0" are set on init. Native <input type="radio"> change events keep all siblings in the same group visually in sync, so labels, click, and form reset all behave correctly.

examples.title: Size group
examples.html:
<label class="brut-radio brut-radio--on" data-brut="radio">
  <input type="radio" name="size" value="md" hidden checked/>
</label>
<label class="brut-radio" data-brut="radio">
  <input type="radio" name="size" value="lg" hidden/>
</label>

components.name: range-dual
components.description: Two-thumb range slider that emits a min/max pair, with pointer drag, click-to-jump on the track, and keyboard nudging.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-min
dataAttributes.description: Lower bound of the slider

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-max
dataAttributes.description: Upper bound of the slider

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-step
dataAttributes.description: Snap increment for thumbs and keyboard

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value-min
dataAttributes.description: Initial position of the lower thumb

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value-max
dataAttributes.description: Initial position of the upper thumb

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name-min
dataAttributes.description: Form name for the auto-created hidden input mirroring the lower value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name-max
dataAttributes.description: Form name for the auto-created hidden input mirroring the upper value

formState.name: two hidden inputs created when data-brut-name-min / data-brut-name-max are set

a11y.notes: The lower thumb is clamped at the upper thumb and vice versa via aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax. PageUp/PageDown move by 10× step. Home/End collapse to the opposite thumb or to min/max.

examples.title: Default — 0 to 100, step 1
Coarse step (5) over a wider range
Year window

components.name: rating
components.description: Row of 1–N star buttons that records an integer score, with hover preview, click-to-toggle, and full keyboard control.

modifiers.name: .brut-rating__star--on
modifiers.description: Filled state applied to stars at or below the current value

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Form name; when set, a hidden input is auto-created and synced

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-value
dataAttributes.description: Initial score; clamped to 0..N where N is the star count

formState.name: hidden input created automatically when data-brut-name is set

a11y.role: slider (wrapper) with role="radio" on each star
a11y.notes: Wrapper is the focusable target with tabindex="0". Hover and focus on individual stars preview the score; mouseleave/focusout repaint the locked value. Clicking the active star clears the score (toggle to 0).

examples.title: Empty (no selection)
Pre-selected (3 of 5)

components.name: search
components.description: Text input wrapper with a clear (×) button that appears only when the field has a value and resets the input on click.

modifiers.name: .brut-search--has-value
modifiers.description: Toggled on the wrapper while the input is non-empty; reveals the clear button

detail.value: string (current input value, native event on the inner input)
string (fires on blur and after the clear button is pressed)

formState.name: the inner <input type="search"> is the canonical form value

a11y.notes: Clearing dispatches both input and change events on the inner field, then refocuses the input so typing can continue uninterrupted.

examples.title: Empty
Pre-filled (clear button visible)

components.name: section
components.description: Full-width page section with vertical padding and a bottom border. Use modifiers for background color themes and spacing adjustments.
components.notes: Sections are full-width by default. Nest a .brut-container inside to constrain content width. Combine background modifiers with --tight or --loose for spacing control.

examples.title: Hero section with dark background
examples.html: HERO TITLE

examples.title: Alternating content sections
examples.html:

components.name: segmented
components.description: Exclusive choice button group that behaves like a horizontal radio set, with roving tabindex and arrow-key navigation.

modifiers.name: .brut-segmented__btn--on
modifiers.description: Active state applied to the currently selected segment button

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Form name; when set, a hidden input is auto-created and synced to the active segment

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Per-button value; falls back to the button's text content when omitted

detail.value: string (data-value of the selected segment, or its text content)

formState.name: hidden input created automatically when data-brut-name is set

a11y.role: tablist with role="tab" on each segment
a11y.notes: Arrow keys wrap at both ends. Roving tabindex keeps exactly one segment focusable; activation moves focus to the new segment.

examples.title: Default — three segments
Form-bound (mirrors to a hidden input)

components.name: sidebar
components.description: Vertical app navigation rail with optional collapsible groups; clicking a button-typed group title toggles its closed state.

modifiers.name: .brut-sidebar__group--closed
modifiers.description: Hides the items beneath a group title and flips the title indicator to "+"

modifiers.name: .brut-sidebar__item--active
modifiers.description: Marks the current page link inside the rail

keyboard: Enter / Space (on a group title button)
a11y.notes: Only group titles authored as <button> become collapsible; <h3> or other elements render the same chrome but stay static. Initial closed state is read from the .brut-sidebar__group--closed class on mount.

components.name: stack
components.description: Vertical flex layout with configurable gap between children. The primary primitive for vertical spacing between elements.
components.notes: The base .brut-stack uses the default gap token (--sp-4). Children are laid out in a column with flex-direction: column. For horizontal layout, use .brut-cluster instead.

examples.title: Form fields
Page content sections
examples.html:

Second block

components.name: stepper
components.description: Numeric input flanked by minus / plus buttons; reads min, max, and step from the inner <input type="number"> and clamps to the step grid.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-step
dataAttributes.description: Direction hint on each button; falls back to first-button=down, second-button=up when omitted

formState.name: the inner <input type="number"> is the canonical form value (no separate hidden input)

a11y.notes: PageUp / PageDown move by 10× step. Programmatic input updates suppress duplicate brut:change emissions while still bubbling native input/change. Out-of-range typed values are clamped and snapped to the step grid relative to min.

examples.title: Default — 0 to 99, step 1
Coarse step (5) over 0–100
Decimal step

components.name: switch
components.description: Boolean toggle backed by a hidden <input type="checkbox"> that posts with the surrounding form; click and Space/Enter both flip state.

modifiers.name: .brut-switch--on
modifiers.description: On state; mirrors the inner checkbox's checked property

formState.name: inner <input type="checkbox" hidden> is the source of truth and posts with the form

a11y.notes: The wrapper is a <label> but its native click-forwards to the inner checkbox is intercepted to prevent double-toggling. Native checkbox change events also fire, so existing form handlers continue to work.

examples.title: Default — off
Default — on
Form-bound with name

components.name: table
components.description: Data table with click-to-sort headers, header select-all checkbox, and per-row select checkboxes.

modifiers.name: --responsive
modifiers.description: Below the small breakpoint, stack rows as cards (requires data-brut-col-label on each td)

modifiers.name: --sticky-head
modifiers.description: thead remains visible while tbody scrolls

modifiers.name: --sticky-col
modifiers.description: First column remains visible during horizontal scroll

dataAttributes.name: data-sort-key
dataAttributes.description: On a th.brut-table__cell--sortable: identifier for the sortable column

dataAttributes.name: data-sort-value
dataAttributes.description: On a td: explicit value used for sorting (falls back to text content)

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-select-all
dataAttributes.description: On a header element: toggles every [data-brut-row-select] in the table

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-row-select
dataAttributes.description: On a tbody row checkbox: marks the row as selectable by the header toggle

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-col-label
dataAttributes.description: On a td (responsive mode): label prefix shown before the cell value when stacked

detail.value: string (sort key) or boolean (when selectAll is true)

formState.name: Row checkboxes own their own form state; the table itself does not mirror to a hidden input

a11y.notes: Sortable headers are tabindex=0, activated by Space or Enter. Numeric values sort numerically; otherwise locale-aware case-insensitive string sort. Select-all syncs every row checkbox and dispatches a real change event on the underlying input.

components.name: table-columns
components.description: Column-visibility menu for a brut-table; injects a checkbox menu and toggles column display via a data attribute.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-table
dataAttributes.description: ID of the .brut-table this control governs

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute for the generated hidden input mirroring the visible columns CSV

dataAttributes.name: data-col
dataAttributes.description: Required on every th and td of the target table; identifies the column for hide/show

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-col-label
dataAttributes.description: Optional override for the menu item label (defaults to th text content)

dataAttributes.name: data-col-hidden
dataAttributes.description: Written to the table by the component; matches the injected attribute-selector CSS rules that hide cells

formState.name: Generated hidden input carries data-brut-cols-state and a comma-separated list of visible column keys

a11y.notes: Reuses the brut-menu component for open/close and keyboard navigation; runs Brut.init on the parent so the dynamically inserted menu wires itself up.

examples.title: Toolbar button governing a table

components.name: table-filter
components.description: Global text filter for a brut-table; hides tbody rows whose combined text fails to match every whitespace-separated token (AND, case-insensitive).

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-table
dataAttributes.description: ID of the .brut-table whose rows are filtered

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: name attribute for the generated hidden input mirroring the query

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-filter-hidden
dataAttributes.description: Written to a row by the component when it is hidden by the current filter

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-hidden-by
dataAttributes.description: Sentinel marking which decorator hid the row, so other decorators can coexist without clobbering each other

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-row-expansion
dataAttributes.description: On a row: opt out of being filtered (used by row expansion patterns)

detail.source: "filter" (re-dispatched on the table to notify pagination etc.)

formState.name: Generated hidden input carries data-brut-filter-state and the current query string

a11y.notes: Filter runs on every input event; expansion rows (data-brut-row-expansion) are skipped. Updates a .brut-table-filter__count element with "X of Y" if present.

examples.title: Search box above a table
examples.html:
Name Role
Alice ChenDesigner
Bob MarshEngineer

examples.title: Custom hidden-input name

components.name: tabs
components.description: One-of-N panel switcher: clicking or arrow-keying a tab reveals the panel whose data-brut-panel matches the tab's data-brut-tab.

modifiers.name: .brut-tab--on
modifiers.description: Active state on the currently selected tab button

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tab
dataAttributes.description: Per-tab key; matched against panels' data-brut-panel

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-panel
dataAttributes.description: Per-panel key; the panel whose key matches the active tab is shown, others receive the hidden attribute

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-panels
dataAttributes.description: Optional root in which to look up panels; defaults to the tablist's parent element

a11y.role: tablist with role="tab" on each trigger
a11y.notes: WAI-ARIA tabs pattern with roving tabindex and arrow-key wrap. Panels are hidden via the native [hidden] attribute, so they are removed from layout and the accessibility tree.

examples.title: Default — three tabs, sibling panels
examples.html:

examples.html:

components.name: tag-input
components.description: Chip-entry field. Enter or comma commits a tag, Backspace at empty input removes the last, and each chip has an x button to delete itself.

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name attribute for the auto-created hidden input that mirrors comma-joined tag values

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.description: Set on each .brut-tag chip to provide the canonical tag value; falls back to chip textContent

formState.name: Auto-creates <input type="hidden"> with comma-joined values; name is data-brut-name or "tags"

a11y.notes: Duplicate values are silently rejected. On blur with non-empty input the pending value is committed. Clicking the wrapper outside the field focuses the field.

examples.title: Empty field
examples.html: Press Enter or comma to add.

examples.title: Pre-populated chips
examples.html: CSS JavaScript UX

examples.title: Many tags with custom name
examples.html: bug feature docs

components.name: theme-switcher
components.description: Radio group that flips the active theme via Brut.theme(); writes [data-theme] on the document root and persists the choice.

modifiers.name: __btn--on
modifiers.description: Applied to the currently selected theme button (paired with aria-checked="true")

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-themes
dataAttributes.description: Theme list to render as buttons; each becomes a radio with that data-value

dataAttributes.name: data-value
dataAttributes.values: string (written by JS on each generated button)
dataAttributes.description: Theme name carried by each rendered button

formState.name: Theme is applied via Brut.theme() and document-root [data-theme]; no form value

a11y.notes:
Empty
is enough — the script generates the buttons. Initial selection mirrors Brut.theme(); arrow keys cycle and immediately apply the focused theme.

examples.title: Default three-way switcher
Custom theme list
Inside a topnav
examples.html: BRUT

components.name: time
components.description: Text-field combobox with a popover hour/minute stepper; emits HH:MM (24-hour ISO) with optional 12-hour AM/PM display and minute snapping.

modifiers.name: --open
modifiers.description: Applied while the popover is visible

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-name
dataAttributes.description: Name for the hidden input mirror; if absent, no hidden input is auto-created

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-mode
dataAttributes.description: Display mode for the hour stepper; output is always 24-hour HH:MM

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-minute-step
dataAttributes.description: Minute snap interval; values not on the grid are rounded to the nearest step

formState.name: Hidden input inside the wrapper carries HH:MM; auto-created when data-brut-name is set, otherwise an existing <input type="hidden"> is reused

a11y.notes: Outside-click closes the popover. The visible field is readonly; values are committed via the steppers. In 12-hour mode an AM/PM segmented control toggles below the row.

examples.title: 24-hour, populated
12-hour with AM/PM
15-minute snap

components.name: toast-host
components.description: Container that anchors transient .brut-toast notifications spawned by the imperative Brut.toast() API; auto-creates itself if absent.

modifiers.name: --top-right
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the top-right corner of the viewport (default)

modifiers.name: --top-left
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the top-left corner of the viewport

modifiers.name: --bottom-right
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the bottom-right corner of the viewport

modifiers.name: --bottom-left
modifiers.description: Anchor host to the bottom-left corner of the viewport

formState.name: No form value; the host renders ephemeral notifications

a11y.role: status / alert (per toast: "alert" for err and warn, "status" otherwise)
keyboard: click the toast x button to dismiss
a11y.notes: Toasts are spawned imperatively via Brut.toast({ kind, message, timeout, host }) where kind is "ok" | "warn" | "err" | "info". Each toast bubbles brut:close on dismissal. If no host element exists when Brut.toast() is called, one is appended to <body> automatically.

examples.title: Top-right host (default)
Bottom-left host
Programmatic spawn (after host is present)

components.name: tooltip
components.description: Hover/focus-triggered ink bubble appended to <body> on show and removed on hide; positioned on one of four sides relative to the trigger.

modifiers.name: --left
modifiers.description: Bubble positioned to the left of the trigger

modifiers.name: --right
modifiers.description: Bubble positioned to the right of the trigger

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tip
dataAttributes.description: Required. The tooltip text shown in the bubble

dataAttributes.name: data-brut-tip-side
dataAttributes.description: Side of the trigger on which to render the bubble

formState.name: Display-only — no form value

aria: aria-describedby (set on trigger while bubble is visible)
a11y.notes: Trigger can be any element; if it is a <button> the script forces type="button". Bubble is created on show and removed on hide; only one bubble per trigger. Position is computed from getBoundingClientRect with an 8px gap.

examples.title: Default top tooltip on a button
examples.html:
<button class="brut-btn brut-btn--primary" type="button"
        data-brut="tooltip" data-brut-tip="Saved at 12:04">SAVE</button>

examples.title: Four sides
On a focusable input
examples.html:
<input class="brut-input" type="text" placeholder="Email"
       data-brut="tooltip"
       data-brut-tip="We never share email"
       data-brut-tip-side="bottom" />

components.name: topnav
components.description: Sticky page header whose only behavior is the mobile burger toggle for the link list; outside-click and Escape close it.

modifiers.name: --open
modifiers.description: Applied while the mobile link drawer is expanded

formState.name: Navigation chrome — no form value

a11y.notes: Burger element is .brut-topnav__burger; auto-typed as type="button". Clicks outside the header or pressing Escape close the open menu. Clicking any .brut-topnav__link also closes (mobile UX).

utilities.description: Control the CSS display property of an element.
Set flex container direction and wrap behavior.
Horizontal alignment of flex or grid children along the main axis.
Vertical alignment of flex or grid children along the cross axis.
Override the parent align-items value for a single flex child.
Flex grow, shrink, fill, and order utilities.
Set gap between flex/grid children. Scale: 0=0, 1=4px, 2=8px, 3=12px, 4=16px, 5=24px, 6=32px.
Set margin on all sides or individual edges. Scale: 0=0, 1=4px, 2=8px, 3=12px, 4=16px, 5=24px, 6=32px, 8=48px. Supports auto.
Set padding on all sides or individual edges. Same scale as margin.
Text alignment, transform, wrapping, and truncation.
utilities.notes: Only text-start, text-center, and text-end have responsive variants.

utilities.description: Control font weight.
Override font size using the type scale.
Override line-height.
Override font family.
Width, height, and viewport sizing utilities.
Control element visibility without affecting layout.
utilities.notes: visually-hidden keeps the element in the accessibility tree but hides it visually.

utilities.description: Set CSS position and edge offsets.
Control how content overflows its container.
Text and background color utilities using design tokens.
Add or remove borders using the ink color at bw-3 weight.
Border-radius, shadows, cursor, and interaction utilities.
